task: Plan Twitter/X strategy for askr pre-launch: decide between posting solutions now vs. building audience through replies to relevant voices
discussion_summary: User has tweeted about problems faced with Claude sessions but hasn't announced solutions yet. Askr is ~1 week from public launch. User rejected posting a solution tweet immediately because the product isn't ready. Instead, user wants to build credibility and reach by replying thoughtfully to threads from @lachygroom, @swyx, @levelsio, @marc_louvion on relevant topics—establishing expertise without premature product plugging. This is a pre-launch audience-building strategy, not a feature-building session.

next_actions.action: Identify 3-5 recent threads from @lachygroom, @swyx, @levelsio, @marc_louvion about Claude context limits, session management, or AI developer workflows. Save links.
next_actions.why: These are the exact audience askr targets. Replies to their threads will surface you to people already interested in this problem space.

next_actions.action: Draft 2-3 reply tweets that add genuine insight (not product mention) to each thread—e.g., workarounds you've discovered, patterns you've noticed, lessons from building askr's checkpoint system.
next_actions.why: Replies compound reach better than standalone posts. Positioning as knowledgeable before launch builds trust and inbound interest.

next_actions.action: Schedule replies to post over next 3-5 days (don't flood). Track engagement and which topics resonate most.
next_actions.why: Staggered posting prevents spam signal. Engagement data informs what to emphasize in the actual launch thread.

next_actions.action: Once askr is launch-ready (1 week), draft the launch thread—but lead with the checkpoint/resume daemon value prop, not the problems. Reference the replies you've already made as proof of expertise.
next_actions.why: By then you'll have established credibility. The launch thread becomes a natural conclusion to the conversation you've already started.

decisions.decision: Do not post a solution/feature announcement tweet now
decisions.reason: Askr is not publicly launchable yet (~1 week out). Premature announcement risks credibility if launch slips or feature changes.

decisions.decision: Focus on replies to existing high-reach threads instead of standalone posts
decisions.reason: Replies to @lachygroom, @swyx, @levelsio, @marc_louvion threads will reach the exact audience already interested in this problem. Higher ROI than cold posts.

user_rejected_decisions.what_was_proposed: Post a tweet announcing the solution: 'both problems I posted about? fixed. built a daemon that watches your claude session...'
user_rejected_decisions.user_signal: I don't want to post a solution, cause askr isn't built yet, I think I am still a week out from public launch.

relational_files.why: README will be the landing page for users who click through from Twitter replies. Needs to be polished before launch thread.

blockers: Askr product not launch-ready yet (~1 week remaining). Cannot announce publicly until core features stable.
